platformDescription: A project management application for modern teams to organize projects, track tasks across Kanban-style boards, and collaborate with team members.

flows.name: Sign In to Your Account
flows.description: Signing in is the first step to access all of TaskFlow's features including your projects, tasks, and team management.

steps.title: Open the login page
steps.instruction: Navigate to the TaskFlow login page. You will see a dark-themed sign-in form with the TaskFlow logo and the tagline 'Project management for modern teams'.
steps.explanation: This is the entry point to the application where you authenticate your identity.

steps.title: Enter your credentials
steps.instruction: In the 'Email address' field, type your email (a demo value 'demo@taskflow.io' is pre-filled). In the 'Password' field, type your password (also pre-filled for demo).
steps.explanation: Your email and password authenticate your access to the workspace.

steps.title: Click Sign in
steps.instruction: Click the blue 'Sign in' button below the password field. You will be redirected to the Dashboard page.
steps.explanation: Submitting the form logs you in and takes you to your project dashboard.

flows.name: Explore the Dashboard
flows.description: The Dashboard is your central hub showing all projects, their progress, and recent team activity. Understanding this page is essential for daily use.

steps.title: Review your project cards
steps.instruction: On the Dashboard page, look at the grid of project cards below the 'Projects' heading. Each card shows a project name (e.g., 'Website Redesign'), a colored status dot, a short description, task count, member count, and a progress bar.
steps.explanation: Project cards give you an at-a-glance overview of all active work and how far along each project is.

steps.title: Check recent activity
steps.instruction: Scroll down below the project cards to the 'Recent Activity' section. This shows a feed of recent actions like task completions, new task creations, and comments from team members.
steps.explanation: The activity feed keeps you informed about what your team is working on without needing to open each project individually.

steps.title: Open a project
steps.instruction: Click on any project card (e.g., the 'Website Redesign' card) to navigate to that project's detailed task board view.
steps.explanation: Clicking a project card takes you to the Kanban-style task board where you can see and manage all tasks within that project.

flows.name: Create a New Project
flows.description: Creating projects is fundamental to organizing your team's work. Each project groups related tasks together with a name, description, and assigned members.

steps.title: Navigate to the New Project page
steps.instruction: Click the blue '+ New Project' button in the top-right corner of the Dashboard page, or click '+ New Project' in the left sidebar navigation.
steps.explanation: This takes you to the project creation form where you define your new project's details.

steps.title: Enter the project name
steps.instruction: In the 'Project Name' text field, type a name for your project (e.g., 'Website Redesign'). This is the name that will appear on the Dashboard.
steps.explanation: A clear project name helps your team quickly identify and find the project.

steps.title: Add a project description
steps.instruction: In the 'Description' textarea, type a brief description of what the project is about (e.g., 'Complete overhaul of the marketing website with new branding.').
steps.explanation: A description gives team members context about the project's goals and scope.

steps.title: Set project visibility
steps.instruction: Click the 'Visibility' dropdown and select one of the three options: 'Team — All members can see', 'Private — Only invited members', or 'Public — Visible to everyone'.
steps.explanation: Visibility controls who in your workspace can see and access this project.

steps.title: Invite members (optional)
steps.instruction: In the 'Invite Members' text field, enter email addresses separated by commas for any team members you want to add. You can skip this and add members later.
steps.explanation: Inviting members ensures the right people have access to collaborate from the start.

steps.title: Create the project
steps.instruction: Click the blue 'Create Project' button at the bottom of the form. You will be redirected to the new project's task board.
steps.explanation: Submitting the form creates your project and takes you directly to its Kanban board where you can start adding tasks.

flows.name: Add a Task to a Project
flows.description: Tasks are the individual work items within a project. Adding tasks with priorities and assignees is how your team tracks and divides work.

steps.title: Open the task creation modal
steps.instruction: On the project page (e.g., 'Website Redesign'), click the blue '+ Add Task' button in the top-right corner. A modal dialog will appear over the page.
steps.explanation: The modal provides a quick way to add tasks without leaving the project board.

steps.title: Enter the task title
steps.instruction: In the 'Task Title' field within the modal, type a clear description of the task (e.g., 'Design new homepage mockup').
steps.explanation: A specific task title helps team members understand exactly what needs to be done.

steps.title: Set the task priority
steps.instruction: Click the 'Priority' dropdown in the modal and select one of the three options: 'Low', 'Medium', or 'High'.
steps.explanation: Priority levels help the team focus on the most important or urgent work first.

steps.title: Assign the task to a team member
steps.instruction: Click the 'Assign to' dropdown in the modal and select a team member from the list (e.g., 'Alice (you)', 'Bob', or 'Carol').
steps.explanation: Assigning a task ensures someone is responsible for completing it and receives relevant notifications.

steps.title: Submit the task
steps.instruction: Click the blue 'Create Task' button at the bottom of the modal. The modal will close and the task will be added to the board.
steps.explanation: Creating the task adds it to the project's To Do column where it can be tracked and moved through the workflow.

flows.name: Invite a Team Member
flows.description: Collaboration is central to TaskFlow. Inviting team members allows them to be assigned tasks, view projects, and contribute to your workspace.

steps.title: Navigate to the Team page
steps.instruction: Click 'Team' (with the people icon) in the left sidebar navigation to go to the Team Members page.
steps.explanation: The Team page is where you manage all workspace members and their roles.

steps.title: Open the invite modal
steps.instruction: Click the blue '+ Invite Member' button in the top-right corner of the Team page. An invitation modal dialog will appear.
steps.explanation: The invite modal lets you send email invitations to new team members.

steps.title: Enter the invitee's email
steps.instruction: In the 'Email Address' field within the modal, type the email address of the person you want to invite (e.g., 'colleague@company.com').
steps.explanation: The email address is used to send the invitation and will become their login credential.

steps.title: Select a role
steps.instruction: Click the 'Role' dropdown in the modal and choose one of: 'Member — Can view and edit projects', 'Admin — Full access including settings', or 'Viewer — Read-only access'.
steps.explanation: Roles control what level of access the invited person will have in your workspace.

steps.title: Send the invitation
steps.instruction: Click the blue 'Send Invite' button at the bottom of the modal. The modal will close and the invited member will appear in the team list with a 'Pending' status.
steps.explanation: The invitation is sent via email, and you can track its status until the person accepts.

flows.name: Update Your Profile and Settings
flows.description: Configuring your profile, notification preferences, and workspace settings ensures TaskFlow works the way you need it to.

steps.title: Navigate to Settings
steps.instruction: Click 'Settings' (with the gear icon) in the left sidebar navigation to open the Settings page.
steps.explanation: The Settings page centralizes all account and workspace configuration options.

steps.title: Update your profile
steps.instruction: In the 'Profile' section at the top, update your 'Full Name' and 'Email' fields as needed, then click the blue 'Save Changes' button.
steps.explanation: Your name is displayed throughout the app on task assignments, comments, and the team page.

steps.title: Configure notifications
steps.instruction: Scroll to the 'Notifications' section. Toggle the switches for 'Email notifications', 'Push notifications', and 'Weekly digest'. Blue means enabled, gray means disabled.
steps.explanation: Notification settings let you control how and when you're alerted about task assignments, mentions, and team activity.

steps.title: Update workspace settings
steps.instruction: Scroll to the 'Workspace' section. Update the 'Workspace Name' and select a 'Default Project Visibility', then click 'Save Changes'.
steps.explanation: Workspace settings affect all team members and control defaults for new projects.
